authority.purpose: freeze the rating-blind feature and InterHub human-envelope computation used by RQ014 recovery lane v2
authority.scope: BL90 loader plus BM90 and BT90 builders for all 16 registered feature families
authority.window_local_state_authority: for RQ014 recovery computation this contract's slice-position-window-first derivative rule supersedes any score-stripped transport-schema or inherited config prose that derives state on a larger branch before temporal slicing

scientific_input_readiness.resolution: evaluate the checksum-bound declassification schema and golden-fixture manifest at G2R preflight; no design-time claim about mutable schema readiness is allowed

schema_profile_gate.requirements: all required rating-blind WOD primitives are present, schema-allowlisted, checksum-bound, and fixture-covered

fixture_gate.requirements: every required fixture has input, expected-output, implementation, and environment SHA-256 bindings and passes exactly

scientific_input_readiness.candidate_state_evidence: the rating-blind structural audit found 476 available scenes; candidates contain 2..21 XY samples while candidate velocity and acceleration arrays are empty
scientific_input_readiness.other_safe_source_evidence: tstar pose is a finite 4x4 list; intent is one of 1/GO_STRAIGHT, 2/GO_LEFT, 3/GO_RIGHT; past XY/velocity/acceleration each have 16 samples; future XYZ each have 20 samples while future velocity/acceleration are empty
required_rating_blind_wod_primitives: candidate XY with the frozen raw-point-zero duplicate flag and geometry hash
past XY plus native past velocity/acceleration retained for provenance and parity audit only, and the tstar ego pose
future ego XYZ retained for rating-blind provenance and preprocessing parity but not consumed by legacy_route_reference_v1
intent integer and intent name restricted to 1/GO_STRAIGHT, 2/GO_LEFT, or 3/GO_RIGHT
safe geometry/intent/pose primitives sufficient for the separately frozen WOD CP/HO/MP/F mapping builder
selected counterpart observed XY and timestamps
required_rating_blind_interhub_primitive: observed XY and timestamps for both key participants
focal reference polyline for both directed roles
scientific_input_readiness.safe_primitive_policy: all primitives must be allowlisted in a successor declassification schema, emitted without score/rank/tie/completeness fields, checksum-bound, and validated before G2R; the score-stripped schema governs transported source positions but its derivative prose is non-normative for RQ014 scientific features
scientific_input_readiness.missing_policy: INELIGIBLE_BLIND; no predictor or envelope value may be computed
required_golden_fixtures_before_g2r: R04N seam plus exact-window-local derivative and no-halo fixture
R10L tstar-anchored interpolation plus exact-window-local derivative and no-halo fixture
one fixture for every CH/LF/HF/TP/TF boundary at each sampling rate

interhub_mapping.rule: exact one-to-one lookup only; no geometry inference, filename inference, or fallback

wod_mapping.source: checksum-bound wod_path_type_mapping.csv materialized rating-blind before G2R from the safe geometry/intent/pose primitives
wod_mapping.rule: after materialization use exact one-to-one lookup only; path_type=NA is never executable and runtime geometry inference or fallback is forbidden

path_type_contract.cross_dataset_semantics: the same canonical CP/HO/MP/F mapping artifact and version must bind both WOD query rows and InterHub envelope rows

wod_reference_contract.role: the sole frozen WOD focal reference builder for all 16 recovery feature families
wod_reference_contract.candidate_scope: scene-level and byte-identical for all three candidates in a scene

inputs.past_xy: finite source past_states XY in source order; the audited rated479 source has 16 rows
inputs.intent_name: uppercase string; expected exact safe values GO_STRAIGHT, GO_LEFT, or GO_RIGHT

past_reference.ensure_p0: append p0 with numpy.vstack iff numpy.linalg.norm(past_ref[-1]-p0)>1e-9

wod_reference_contract.focal_output: numpy.vstack([past_ref,forward]) passed as the focal MotionSequence reference

counterpart_reference.builder: the observed counterpart_state[:,0:2] on the exact resampled estimator-window grid
counterpart_reference.candidate_scope: shared across the three candidates when their exact temporal windows coincide

wod_reference_contract.estimator_reference_preparation: pass the raw array to the pinned estimate_ipv_pair implementation, which applies its pinned smooth_ployline reference preparation exactly once

required_bindings: fixture input and expected raw-reference SHA-256

eligibility: both observed XY series finite and timestamp-unique
path type is exactly one of CP/HO/MP/F
both focal reference polylines are present and finite
registered query window lies wholly inside common observed support

directed_roles_in_order.focal_raw_reference: first participant's checksum-bound route/lane reference polyline
directed_roles_in_order.counterpart_raw_reference: second participant's observed resampled window XY

directed_roles_in_order.focal_raw_reference: second participant's checksum-bound route/lane reference polyline
directed_roles_in_order.counterpart_raw_reference: first participant's observed resampled window XY

episode_and_role_contract.reference_preparation: for each directed role pass both raw reference arrays to the pinned estimator and apply pinned smooth_ployline exactly once to each; pre-smoothed input or a second smoothing pass is forbidden
episode_and_role_contract.counterpart_output: computed internally only if required by the pinned pair kernel and always discarded; it never enters the human envelope
episode_and_role_contract.role_requirement: both directed roles must be executable for an episode-query to contribute
episode_and_role_contract.role_weighting: the two roles share the episode's total weight equally before pseudo-anchor and tau weighting

timeline_and_state_contract.position_authority: positions are authoritative for both WOD and InterHub; source candidate velocity/acceleration must not be used

wod_tstar.history_side: resampled ego history includes t<=0 and the last history state is exactly t=0
wod_tstar.candidate_side: effective candidate future begins at t=0.25 after the frozen optional raw-point-zero duplicate removal
wod_tstar.seam: one t=0 state from ego history followed by candidate states with t>0; a candidate t=0 state is forbidden
wod_tstar.counterpart: interpolate the selected observed counterpart to the same absolute relative-time grid inside support only

interhub_pseudo_tstar.origin: episode_grid_origin_ns from the checksum-bound source manifest
interhub_pseudo_tstar.anchor_set_for_query: every grid timestamp for which both directed roles and both participants contain every closed-interval grid point required by that exact query
interhub_pseudo_tstar.selection: all eligible pseudo anchors; no event, outcome, score, or IPV-dependent selection

sampling_definitions.wod_grid_phase: tstar plus integer multiples of 0.25 s
sampling_definitions.wod_future_rule: use the exported effective candidate samples at +0.25,+0.50,... without resampling candidate XY
sampling_definitions.wod_history_rule: linearly interpolate history XY inside support to nonpositive grid ticks and retain the exact t=0 endpoint
sampling_definitions.interhub_rule: linearly interpolate both participant XY series inside support to episode_grid_origin_ns plus integer multiples of 0.25 s

sampling_definitions.wod_grid_phase: tstar plus integer multiples of 0.1 s
sampling_definitions.wod_rule: linearly interpolate XY by absolute relative timestamp inside support after duplicate removal
sampling_definitions.seam_interpolation: for 0<t<0.25 interpolate between the t=0 ego-history XY and the first effective candidate XY at t=0.25
sampling_definitions.interhub_rule: linearly interpolate both participant XY series inside support to episode_grid_origin_ns plus integer multiples of 0.1 s

timeline_and_state_contract.linear_interpolation: componentwise p(t)=p0+(t-t0)/(t1-t0)*(p1-p0) using the immediately bracketing source timestamps; exact source timestamps retain their source XY

maximum_source_gap.default: at most 2*dt between bracketing source timestamps; a larger gap makes the query ineligible
maximum_source_gap.R04N_wod_candidate: exact native 0.25 s consecutive effective samples; no candidate XY interpolation
maximum_source_gap.R10L_wod_candidate: at most the registered native candidate cadence 0.25 s, including the t=0 history-to-first-candidate seam; this explicit exception permits the intended 0.1 s interpolation grid

derived_state_rule.stage: for every WOD or InterHub estimator query, resolve the exact closed temporal interval, slice that interval's resampled position rows first, and only then derive state inside that window
derived_state_rule.position_window_input: exact ordered positions p[0]..p[n-1] at every closed grid tick from the registered lower_tick through upper_tick inclusive; require n>=2
derived_state_rule.acceleration: inside the same sliced window apply the identical first-one-sided/interior-centered/last-one-sided finite-difference operator to that window's derived velocity only
derived_state_rule.heading: inside the same sliced window set atan2(vy,vx) when hypot(vx,vy)>1e-9; otherwise copy only the nearest earlier defined heading in that window and backfill leading undefined ticks only from the first later defined heading in that window
derived_state_rule.angle_storage: radians in (-pi,pi] without smoothing or unwrapping; an atan2 result exactly equal to -pi is normalized to +pi before storage, comparison, or hashing
derived_state_rule.negative_pi_normalization: if heading == -math.pi set heading = +math.pi; no tolerance-based conversion
derived_state_rule.derivative_halo: FORBIDDEN: do not read any position tick below the window lower_tick or above its upper_tick
derived_state_rule.cross_window_state_reuse: FORBIDDEN: do not copy velocity, acceleration, or heading derived for a larger, smaller, earlier, later, or overlapping window
derived_state_rule.endpoint_consequence: the same physical position tick may intentionally receive different endpoint versus interior derived state in different registered windows
derived_state_rule.source_dynamics: source velocity and acceleration columns are provenance-only and never replace this window-local operator
derived_state_rule.reference_separation: MotionSequence state positions and all derivatives obey the exact window; the separately frozen scene-level reference polyline remains non-state route context and never authorizes a derivative halo or extra observed-track row

timeline_and_state_contract.common_end: for an eligible scene H_common is the finite nonnegative integer grid tick equal to the largest tick jointly supported by all three WOD candidates and the selected counterpart after sampling; no extrapolation

temporal_position_support_contract.closed_window_only: the MotionSequence observed position/state rows are exactly the registered closed interval and no outside position is consumed for state derivation
temporal_position_support_contract.CH: CH-W10 and CH-W25 consume no observed-track position later than tau
temporal_position_support_contract.TP: TP consumes no observed-track position later than tau
temporal_position_support_contract.LF: LF-W10 and LF-W25 consume observed-track positions only from tau through tau+W inclusive and no earlier or later position
temporal_position_support_contract.HF: HF-W10 and HF-W25 consume observed-track positions only from tau-W through tau+W inclusive
temporal_position_support_contract.TF: TF consumes observed-track positions only from tstar=0 through H_common inclusive
temporal_position_support_contract.references: raw reference polylines are separate frozen route context, never MotionSequence observed rows and never derivative support

temporal_recipes.tau_anchor_rule: the position window is tau-invariant, but every tau_tick is supplied only by horizon_anchor_contract and never exceeds h_common_tick

horizon_anchor_contract.tick_units: integer ticks at the feature family's rate_hz with tstar_tick=0
horizon_anchor_contract.window_completeness: for a candidate tau_tick first require tau_tick<=h_common_tick for every temporal recipe, then evaluate the selected recipe's exact lower_tick and upper_tick formulas and retain the anchor only when every closed position tick lies inside four-way observed support; derivative halos do not enter this predicate

H20.requirement: require 2*rate_hz<=h_common_tick and every candidate tick's exact temporal window complete; otherwise the scene/predictor query is INELIGIBLE_TIMELINE_SUPPORT and no shortened H20 set is emitted

HFEAS.candidate_tau_ticks: integer closed interval rate_hz..h_common_tick, empty when h_common_tick<rate_hz
HFEAS.filter: retain only candidate ticks satisfying window_completeness
HFEAS.maximum_tau_tick: h_common_tick for CH, LF, HF, TP, and TF
HFEAS.TF: when [0,h_common_tick] is complete, retained TF anchors are exactly every integer tick rate_hz..h_common_tick; the tau-invariant TF window never creates anchors beyond h_common_tick
HFEAS.minimum_requirement: the complete H20 anchor set must exist

horizon_anchor_contract.finite_domain_proof: each horizon emits a subset of one finite integer closed interval, so every feature/horizon scene has a finite tau list and a unique maximum valid tau when nonempty
horizon_anchor_contract.domain_materialization: materialize every segment_id x feature_id x horizon_id result exactly once through scene_anchor_domain_contract before feature evaluation, readout computation, or envelope-query aggregation

scene_anchor_domain_contract.purpose: checksum-bound reversible per-scene membership authority; aggregate query rows never substitute for this artifact
scene_anchor_domain_contract.status_scope: membership_status terminates only rating-blind path/timeline/window/state construction for the scene-feature-horizon anchor domain; downstream IPV solver and envelope outcomes remain separate registered execution statuses and never rewrite this immutable membership artifact
scene_anchor_domain_contract.expected_group_count: 479*16*2=15328 segment_id x feature_id x horizon_id groups
scene_anchor_domain_contract.encoding: CPython 3.9 csv.writer with dialect=excel, quoting=csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL, lineterminator='\n', UTF-8 without BOM; header and every data row end in exactly one LF and CR is forbidden
scene_anchor_domain_contract.row_order: segment_id raw UTF-8 bytes ascending, feature_id by feature_families order, horizon_id H20 then HFEAS, then AVAILABLE tau_tick integer ascending; an ineligible group has only its literal NA tau row

field_rules.path_type_or_NA: canonical CP/HO/MP/F when the checksum-bound WOD lookup succeeds, otherwise literal NA; every AVAILABLE row in every group for that segment must repeat the unique value returned by the exact checksum-bound (segment_id,tstar_context_step) lookup
field_rules.h_common_tick_or_NA: for TF use the finite scene h_common_tick whenever it was resolved, otherwise literal NA; for every non-TF feature use literal NA because h_common_tick is not an envelope lookup dimension
field_rules.tau_tick_or_NA: base-10 nonnegative JSON-style integer text for an AVAILABLE membership row, or literal NA for the sole terminal row
field_rules.membership_status: one exact value from registered_membership_statuses; envelope-only, InterHub-only, solver-result, rating, or runtime-invented statuses are forbidden
field_rules.reason_code: F_AVAILABLE_CONTINUE for AVAILABLE, otherwise the unique reason_code mapped from membership_status by recovery_lane_v2.upstream_terminal_rollup

scene_anchor_domain_contract.available_group: one or more rows, every row membership_status=AVAILABLE and reason_code=F_AVAILABLE_CONTINUE; set(path_type_or_NA) has cardinality exactly 1 and its sole CP/HO/MP/F value equals the exact checksum-bound WOD path lookup for that segment's frozen (segment_id,tstar_context_step); tau_tick_or_NA values are unique integers exactly equal to the frozen H20 or HFEAS membership and sorted ascending; TF requires set(h_common_tick_or_NA) cardinality exactly 1 with one finite integer and non-TF requires literal NA on every row; terminal rows are forbidden

path_type_group_invariant.expected_value: the unique CP/HO/MP/F returned by path_type_contract.wod_mapping for this segment's frozen (segment_id,tstar_context_step)
path_type_group_invariant.cross_group_scene_binding: every AVAILABLE feature_id x horizon_id group for the same segment_id repeats that same checksum-bound lookup value

scene_anchor_domain_contract.terminal_group: exactly one row with tau_tick_or_NA=NA, one non-AVAILABLE registered_membership_status and its unique registered reason_code; AVAILABLE rows are forbidden
scene_anchor_domain_contract.horizon_binding: AVAILABLE memberships are exactly the ticks emitted by horizon_anchor_contract; H20 and HFEAS are not regenerated by any downstream consumer

manifest.serialization: CPython 3.9 sorted-key compact canonical JSON plus exactly one terminal LF, UTF-8 without BOM, allow_nan=False

forbidden: derive a scene membership from required_envelope_queries.csv
mix AVAILABLE and terminal rows in one group
share one scene membership row set between distinct group keys

estimator_call_contract.reported_value: focal-agent IPV at the sole terminal estimator row

wod_query_manifest.source_artifact: only checksum-verified AVAILABLE rows from wod_scene_anchor_domain.csv under its exact manifest
wod_query_manifest.encoding: CPython 3.9 csv.writer with dialect=excel, quoting=csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL, lineterminator='\n', UTF-8 without BOM and no CR
wod_query_manifest.rule: rename path_type_or_NA to path_type and tau_tick_or_NA to tau_tick after requiring non-NA values, then perform the one unique group-by over feature_id,path_type,horizon_id,tau_tick,h_common_tick_or_NA; set wod_scene_count=count(distinct segment_id); sort by feature_families order, CP/HO/MP/F order, H20/HFEAS order, tau_tick integer ascending, then h_common_tick integer with literal NA before integers
wod_query_manifest.group_by_identity: every AVAILABLE scene-membership row contributes to exactly one aggregate key and wod_scene_count is exactly its distinct segment cardinality; because each AVAILABLE scene-feature-horizon group has path_type set cardinality exactly 1, all of that group's tau rows contribute only to that one checksum-bound path_type branch; terminal rows contribute to none
wod_query_manifest.aggregate_only: this table contains no segment_id and cannot identify, reconstruct, extend, intersect, or substitute for any per-scene anchor membership
wod_query_manifest.domain_binding: WOD feature evaluation and per-scene readouts consume wod_scene_anchor_domain.csv directly; BM90/BT90 query generation consumes only this unique aggregate, which is itself derived once from that scene artifact
wod_query_manifest.manifest_binding: the envelope builder manifest binds exact path/size/SHA-256 for both wod_scene_anchor_domain.csv and its manifest plus required_envelope_queries.csv and its aggregate-generator implementation

weighted_quantile_contract.rule: left-continuous inverse of the normalized weighted empirical CDF
weighted_quantile_contract.algorithm: sort by stable_sort_keys ascending; weight_total=math.fsum(weights in sorted order); require finite weight_total>0; normalized_weight=weight/weight_total; scan in sorted order with cumulative=cumulative+normalized_weight using binary64 addition from initial 0.0; return the first IPV whose cumulative>=q
weighted_quantile_contract.key_comparison: numeric fields numeric ascending; episode_id raw UTF-8 bytes ascending; role_id order A_AS_FOCAL then B_AS_FOCAL; literal NA is only compared with NA inside one cell
weighted_quantile_contract.episode_order_before_weighting: unique episode_id raw UTF-8 bytes ascending
weighted_quantile_contract.episode_weight: every contributing episode has total weight 1/E within the exact envelope cell
weighted_quantile_contract.within_episode_weight: for episode e let K_e be its total count of eligible IPV observations across both required directed roles and all pseudo-anchor/query observations in the exact cell; require integer K_e>0 and give each observation raw weight (1/E)/K_e

envelope_builders.execution: load the exact checksum-bound legacy L/M/U row by path_type; never reconstruct, refit, smooth, or imitate
envelope_builders.family_application: the same exact legacy path-type row is applied to every registered feature family as an intentionally historical scale candidate
envelope_builders.gate: exact artifact, builder provenance, source manifest, path mapping, finite ordered L<M<U, and hashes all verified

legacy_artifact_contract.artifact_serialization: CPython 3.9 json.dumps with sort_keys=True,separators=(',',':'),ensure_ascii=False,allow_nan=False plus exactly one terminal LF, UTF-8 without BOM
legacy_artifact_contract.binary64_encoding: each bound is exactly 16 lowercase hexadecimal digits encoding its IEEE-754 binary64 big-endian bytes; decode with struct.unpack('>d',bytes.fromhex(value))[0] and require finite L<M<U
legacy_artifact_contract.row_hash_preimage: ASCII RQ014-BL90-ROW| concatenated with path_type strict UTF-8, one ASCII |, then raw 8-byte L, raw 8-byte M, and raw 8-byte U in that order; row_canonical_sha256 is lowercase SHA256 hex of this preimage
legacy_artifact_contract.manifest_serialization: the same artifact_serialization canonical JSON rule
legacy_artifact_contract.screen_binding: the blind feature-bank manifest and every BL90 execution receipt bind the exact artifact path/size/SHA-256 and manifest path/size/SHA-256 used by the screen
legacy_artifact_contract.selected_recipe_binding: when BL90 is selected, selected_recovery_recipe.json copies the exact artifact and manifest bindings plus every path-type row actually present in selected support, in canonical CP/HO/MP/F order, with its L/M/U hex and row_canonical_sha256
legacy_artifact_contract.clean_replay_rule: the replay allowlist admits only that exact artifact and manifest; verify both hashes and every selected row byte/hash before loading; reconstruction, imitation, screen-cache substitution, or a different legacy artifact is forbidden
legacy_artifact_contract.rankability: BL90 rows are AVAILABLE and rankable only after the artifact, manifest, source, builder, mapping, all required selected-support path rows, and hashes pass; otherwise every affected BL90 predictor cell is INELIGIBLE_REFERENCE

envelope_builders.execution: build episode-equal weighted q05/q50/q95 from the exact family estimator over the registered horizon pooling domain
envelope_builders.query_source: consume only the unique checksum-bound required_envelope_queries.csv aggregate; independent horizon/tau regeneration or per-scene inference is forbidden

pooling_domain.H20: all and only complete required tau ticks rate_hz..2*rate_hz from horizon_anchor_contract for that sampling rate
pooling_domain.HFEAS: the sorted unique finite required tau ticks rate_hz..h_common_tick in the frozen WOD query manifest for that feature/path type
pooling_domain.TF: never pool different h_common_tick values; h_common_tick is an exact lookup dimension
pooling_domain.other_temporal_modes: pool all eligible pseudo-anchor, role, and registered tau observations inside the exact feature/path/horizon cell

envelope_builders.episode_weighting: one total unit per episode within the exact lookup cell regardless of roles, pseudo-anchor count, or tau count
envelope_builders.output: one L/M/U row for each required BM90 lookup key

envelope_builders.execution: build episode-equal weighted q05/q50/q95 at the exact registered tau tick
envelope_builders.query_source: consume only the unique checksum-bound required_envelope_queries.csv aggregate; independent horizon/tau regeneration or per-scene inference is forbidden
envelope_builders.tau_cell: one cell per exact integer tau tick; interpolation between envelope tau cells is forbidden
envelope_builders.horizon_pooling: none; H20 and HFEAS reuse byte-identical BT90 rows when their lookup keys coincide
envelope_builders.TF: never pool different h_common_tick values and never materialize a TF tau cell above its h_common_tick
envelope_builders.episode_weighting: one total unit per episode within the exact tau cell regardless of roles or pseudo-anchor count
envelope_builders.output: one L/M/U row for each required BT90 lookup key

BM90_values.h_common_tick_or_NA: integer tick for TF, otherwise literal string NA
BM90_values.path_type: exact CP, HO, MP, or F

BT90_values.h_common_tick_or_NA: integer tick for TF, otherwise literal string NA
BT90_values.path_type: exact CP, HO, MP, or F

bootstrap_canonical_lookup.NA_encoding: the literal JSON string "NA" (four UTF-8 bytes including quotes; two ASCII content bytes); JSON null is forbidden
bootstrap_canonical_lookup.tick_encoding: base-10 JSON integer; floats, exponent notation, and quoted integers are forbidden
bootstrap_canonical_lookup.serialization: json.dumps(object,sort_keys=True,separators=(',',':'),ensure_ascii=False,allow_nan=False) followed by exactly one LF byte
bootstrap_canonical_lookup.bytes: UTF-8 with no BOM and exactly one terminal byte 0x0A
bootstrap_canonical_lookup.seed_material: ASCII bytes RQ014-ENVBOOT| concatenated directly with canonical lookup UTF-8-LF bytes

bootstrap.unit: episode within the exact envelope cell
bootstrap.episode_source_order: sort unique episode_id values by raw UTF-8 byte order ascending before assigning integer positions 0..E-1
bootstrap.draw_rule: for replicate_index 0..999 in increasing order call rng.integers(0,E,size=E,dtype=numpy.int64,endpoint=False) exactly once; no discarded, warm-up, vectorized multi-replicate, or parallel RNG draws
bootstrap.draw_order: preserve returned draw_position 0..E-1; sampled occurrence identity is (replicate_index,draw_position,source_episode_id)

occurrence_identity_schema.source_episode_id: exact source UTF-8 identifier at the sampled source position

bootstrap.occurrence_order_rule: within a replicate retain draw_position order when assigning occurrence weights and identities; repeated source_episode_id values remain distinct occurrences and are never collapsed or re-sorted before bootstrap_quantile_sort_keys are applied
bootstrap.resampling: every sampled episode occurrence is an independent weighting group with total raw weight 1/E and retains both directed roles plus all source-episode observations in the exact cell; repeated source episodes remain distinct occurrences even though their IPV payloads are byte-identical
bootstrap.occurrence_observation_weight: for draw occurrence j sampling source episode e_j with K_e_j eligible observations, assign each retained observation raw weight (1/E)/K_e_j; compute K_e_j separately within the source episode before sampling and reuse its exact positive integer for every repeated occurrence
bootstrap.weight_normalization_order: materialize all E occurrence groups and their observation raw weights, stable-sort observations once by bootstrap_quantile_sort_keys, compute weight_total=math.fsum(raw weights in that sorted order), require finite weight_total>0, divide each raw weight by weight_total in sorted order, then run the weighted_quantile_contract cumulative scan in that same order
bootstrap.fixed_draw_count_and_retry_policy: execute exactly the 1000 registered RNG calls and no others; an invalid replicate is retained as failed metadata and is never retried, replaced, discarded, or followed by an extra draw
bootstrap.replicate_success_predicate: one of the fixed 1000 replicates is successful iff it contains at least one retained observation and every IPV, raw/normalized weight, weight_total, q05/q50/q95, and both derived half-widths is finite; strict_positive_weight_invariant separately guarantees all weights and weight_total are >0
bootstrap.finite_degenerate_replicates: fewer than 3 distinct resampled IPV values, q05=q50, q50=q95, q05=q50=q95, and either or both replicate half-widths equal to 0.0 are valid successful finite width observations; they are not retried or discarded
bootstrap.strict_positive_weight_invariant: because E>0 and every K_e>0, every constructed raw weight and finite weight_total must be strictly positive; a finite nonpositive value is FATAL_BOOTSTRAP_WEIGHTING_ERROR and aborts the envelope build rather than becoming a failed or replacement replicate
bootstrap.replicate_failure_predicate: empty retained observation set or any nonfinite IPV/weight/weight_total/quantile/half-width; these are the only replicate failures
bootstrap.half_width_relative_se: only after all fixed 1000 replicates satisfy replicate_success_predicate, for each side set mean_width=math.fsum(widths including valid zero widths in replicate order)/1000 once, sample_sd=math.sqrt(math.fsum((width_b-mean_width)**2 in replicate order)/999), then sample_sd/original_half_width; the original gate guarantees original_half_width>=1e-6
bootstrap.successful_replicate_count: count successful statuses over exactly replicate_index 0..999; any value other than 1000 yields INELIGIBLE_ENVELOPE_UNCERTAIN and no replacement draw
bootstrap.implementation_gate: bootstrap implementation, numpy version, Python executable, environment manifest, canonical lookup fixtures, first-draw fixture, quantile fixture, and relative-SE fixture SHA-256 values are frozen before G2R; any drift is INELIGIBLE_BLIND

envelope_gate.required_query_failure: materialize every required lookup row and retain its granular terminal status; the enclosing predictor cell selects the registered first granular failure through the recovery-lane rollup priority before rating access; INELIGIBLE_ENVELOPE_REQUIRED_CELL is reserved for an absent required lookup row, not a wrapper that erases low-count/degenerate/uncertain reasons

terminal_status_completeness.identity: set(terminal_statuses) equals {AVAILABLE} union the exact values at producer_paths after wildcard expansion; duplicate list entries, missing producer values, and unused list values are FATAL_CONTRACT_ERROR

execution_enumeration.unique_executable_rule: each of the 16x3 execution IDs resolves to exactly one sampling definition, temporal recipe, estimator call, role rule, path-type rule, builder/loader, weighting rule, gate, and missing-state rule

manifest_required_bindings: Python executable and environment manifest SHA-256
